pages.title: Neuroartan Docs

hero.title: Help for wherever you are on your Neuroartan journey.
hero.description: Search Neuroartan documentation or browse the main documentation areas below.

pages.title: Products
pages.description: Neuroartan is built around ICOS: a cognitive operating system for personal continuity, memory, voice, reflection, profile identity, publishing, and governed model development.

hero.title: Products
hero.description: Neuroartan is built around ICOS: a cognitive operating system for personal continuity, memory, voice, reflection, profile identity, publishing, and governed model development.

sections.title: Product direction
sections.body: Neuroartan’s product direction begins with ICOS. ICOS is not positioned as a generic chatbot. It is a governed cognitive operating system focused on continuity, memory, reflection, profile identity, voice, publishing, and long-term personal model development.

sections.title: Core product surfaces
sections.body: The core product surfaces include Thought Bank, Posts, Profile, Public Profile, Memory, Voice Training, Continuity, Model Training, Publishing, Settings, and Capabilities. These surfaces should work together as one product architecture rather than isolated features.

sections.title: Trust boundary
sections.body: Product capabilities must remain aligned with privacy, verification, anti-impersonation, device integrity, safety, appeal, and data retention boundaries. Payment may increase depth and support pathways, but it must not bypass trust, safety, privacy, or legal review.

pages.title: ICOS
pages.description: ICOS is Neuroartan’s cognitive operating system for personal continuity, memory, reflection, voice, profile identity, publishing, and governed model development.

hero.title: ICOS
hero.description: ICOS is Neuroartan’s cognitive operating system for personal continuity, memory, reflection, voice, profile identity, publishing, and governed model development.

sections.title: Product definition
sections.body: ICOS is the Intelligence Cognitive Operating System: a governed personal continuity system that helps a user capture thought, develop memory, refine voice, structure reflection, manage profile identity, and build one canonical personal model over time.

sections.title: Not a generic chatbot
sections.body: ICOS should not be framed as a generic chatbot. It is a continuity-centered cognitive system. The product value comes from structured memory, personal model fidelity, correction, voice alignment, privacy controls, publishing boundaries, and long-term profile coherence.

sections.title: Trust and safety
sections.body: ICOS must remain aligned with privacy, verification, anti-impersonation, abuse prevention, device integrity, data retention, and appeal boundaries. Product depth must not bypass safety review, verification review, identity protection, or legal requirements.

pages.title: Thought Bank
pages.description: Thought Bank is the private cognitive substrate inside ICOS for thoughts, reflections, corrections, raw ideas, and continuity signals.

hero.title: Thought Bank
hero.description: Thought Bank is the private cognitive substrate inside ICOS. It is where thoughts, reflections, corrections, raw ideas, and continuity signals can be captured before any public expression is created.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Thought Bank exists to give users a private place to capture thought before it becomes publication, memory, profile expression, or structured output. It is a thinking surface, not a public feed.

sections.title: Private by design
sections.body: Thought Bank should remain private by default. Private entries should not appear as public Posts, public profile material, search-visible content, or externally visible publications unless the user explicitly chooses a publishing path through approved controls.

sections.title: Relationship to Posts
sections.body: Thought Bank and Posts are separate product layers. Thought Bank captures private cognition. Posts publish selected expression. This separation protects privacy, reduces accidental disclosure, and gives the user control over what becomes public.

pages.title: Posts
pages.description: Posts are the expression and publishing layer of ICOS, separate from private Thought Bank material.

hero.title: Posts
hero.description: Posts are the expression and publishing layer of ICOS. They are separate from private Thought Bank material and should publish only through deliberate user action and approved visibility controls.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Posts exist to give users a controlled way to publish selected expression from their broader ICOS environment. They are not the same as Thought Bank. A post should represent deliberate public or visibility-controlled expression, not automatic exposure of private thought.

sections.title: Publishing action
sections.body: A post should require an intentional publishing action. Publishing workflows should make visibility, profile association, edit state, and public impact clear before content becomes visible outside the private workspace.

sections.title: Safety and moderation
sections.body: Posts should remain subject to safety, anti-impersonation, abuse-prevention, privacy, copyright, legal, and platform integrity rules.

pages.title: Memory
pages.description: Memory gives ICOS continuity through approved context, corrections, preferences, source references, and long-term patterns.

hero.title: Memory
hero.description: Memory gives ICOS continuity. It preserves approved context, corrections, preferences, profile signals, source references, and long-term patterns so one canonical personal model can become more faithful over time.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Memory exists to make ICOS continuous rather than session-bound. It helps the system remember approved user context, preferences, corrections, source references, profile signals, and long-term patterns so responses can become more aligned with the user over time.

sections.title: Approved memory
sections.body: Memory should be built from approved sources, user actions, profile settings, corrections, preferences, Posts where allowed, Thought Bank signals where authorized, voice and style training, and other permitted continuity records.

sections.title: One canonical model
sections.body: Memory should deepen one canonical personal model. It should not create multiple personal models, duplicate identities, impersonation models, or unmanaged model copies.

pages.title: Developers
pages.description: Developer documentation for APIs, OAuth connectors, CLI, troubleshooting, public error IDs, security requirements, release notes, and support-safe diagnostics.

hero.title: Developers
hero.description: Developer documentation explains platform integration, OAuth connectors, API behavior, CLI direction, troubleshooting, release notes, security requirements, and public error IDs.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Developer documentation gives engineers and technical users public-safe guidance for integrating with Neuroartan systems without exposing private infrastructure, secrets, security-sensitive implementation details, or abuse-sensitive mechanics.

sections.title: Developer surfaces
sections.body: Developer surfaces include APIs, OAuth connectors, CLI workflows, troubleshooting, error references, security requirements, release notes, and support-safe diagnostic output.

sections.title: Security boundary
sections.body: Developer documentation must not expose secrets, private endpoints, service keys, fraud signals, reviewer notes, private database identifiers, or security thresholds.

pages.title: Support
pages.description: Support routing for account help, product questions, privacy requests, verification review, developer issues, billing direction, safety notices, and escalation.

hero.title: Support
hero.description: Support documentation helps users find the correct path for account help, product questions, privacy requests, verification review, developer issues, billing direction, safety notices, and escalation.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Support exists to route users to the correct help path. It should separate account help, product help, privacy requests, verification review, developer issues, billing direction, safety notices, and legal notices so each request can be handled by the correct process.

sections.title: Support principle
sections.body: Support should be clear, searchable, public-safe, and escalation-aware. It should help users resolve ordinary issues quickly while protecting private data, security systems, reviewer notes, device integrity signals, fraud controls, and legal-sensitive information.

sections.title: Escalation boundary
sections.body: Some issues may require support, privacy review, legal review, security review, verification review, safety review, or manual account review. Support documentation should identify the escalation path without promising a specific outcome.

pages.title: Investors
pages.description: Investor relations direction for product thesis, market category, revenue direction, trust architecture, public roadmap, and disclosure boundaries.

hero.title: Investors
hero.description: Neuroartan is building ICOS: a governed cognitive operating system for continuity, memory, voice, profile identity, publishing, and long-term personal model development.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: This page gives investors a public-facing overview of Neuroartan’s product direction, commercial logic, trust architecture, and current disclosure boundary. It is informational and does not replace a private investor data room, securities document, legal review, or financial disclosure.

sections.title: Product thesis
sections.body: The product thesis is that personal AI value increases when the system develops continuity with the user: approved memory, corrections, voice, style, profile identity, private Thought Bank structure, public Posts, and one canonical personal model that becomes more faithful over time.

sections.title: Investor relations boundary
sections.body: Financial projections, fundraising terms, valuation, legal entity details, securities disclosures, cap table information, private roadmap material, and investor data-room access require executive, finance, legal, and governance approval.

pages.title: Press
pages.description: Press documentation defines how Neuroartan should be described publicly: a company building ICOS, a cognitive operating system for continuity, memory, voice, profile identity, publishing, and governed personal model development.

hero.title: Press
hero.description: Press documentation defines how Neuroartan should be described publicly: a company building ICOS, a cognitive operating system for continuity, memory, voice, profile identity, publishing, and governed personal model development.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: This page gives press and public audiences a concise, accurate way to understand Neuroartan. It defines company language, product language, approved public framing, claim boundaries, media-use boundaries, and the review status for public communication.

sections.title: Company description
sections.body: Neuroartan is building ICOS, a cognitive operating system for personal continuity, memory, voice, profile identity, publishing, and governed personal model development. The company’s public language should remain clear, restrained, product-centered, and trust-aware.

sections.title: Claim boundaries
sections.body: Public claims should not promise medical, therapeutic, legal, financial, educational, employment, verification, safety, investment, marketplace, income, or product-availability outcomes unless those claims are separately approved and legally supported.

pages.title: Jobs
pages.description: Neuroartan hiring will support ICOS across product, engineering, design, governance, privacy, trust and safety, developer systems, support, communications, and operations.

hero.title: Jobs
hero.description: Neuroartan hiring will support ICOS across product, engineering, design, governance, privacy, trust and safety, developer systems, support, communications, and operations.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: This page defines public-facing hiring direction for Neuroartan. It is not a list of currently open positions unless a role is explicitly published as open. It explains the kinds of functions Neuroartan expects to need as ICOS matures.

sections.title: Hiring direction
sections.body: Future hiring should support a careful product build: ICOS, memory, continuity, voice training, profile identity, publishing, verification, privacy, safety, developer systems, support infrastructure, design systems, and public communication.

sections.title: Application boundary
sections.body: Applications should route through the official hiring channel once active. Public job language should not imply employment guarantees, visa sponsorship, contractor terms, compensation, equity, start dates, or remote-work policy unless those details are approved for the specific role.

pages.title: Privacy
pages.description: Privacy documentation for memory and continuity controls, Thought Bank privacy, verification evidence, device integrity, data retention, local-first architecture, data ownership, voice, legacy continuity, assisted onboarding boundaries, user rights, and public-safe privacy boundaries.

hero.title: Privacy
hero.description: Privacy documentation explains local-first architecture, memory, profiles, Thought Bank, Posts, voice, legacy continuity, assisted onboarding, verification, device integrity, data retention, user controls, and review boundaries.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Privacy documentation explains public-safe privacy concepts in clear language, including local-first vault direction, iCloud-aware storage, encrypted cloud processing, memory, voice, model calibration, legacy continuity, and user controls.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.body: Neuroartan’s direction is that the user owns the raw mind archive while Neuroartan provides the governed intelligence, consent, indexing, and optional encrypted processing layer.

sections.title: Data rights
sections.body: Users should be able to understand, access, correct, export, restrict, and delete sensitive data where technically and legally possible.

sections.title: Boundary
sections.body: Private memory, Thought Bank entries, voice material, legacy settings, verification evidence, and internal review notes must remain protected. Public documentation describes direction and may include capabilities that require legal, security, and implementation review before production.

sections.title: Principle

sections.title: Purpose

sections.title: Boundary

sections.title: Purpose

sections.body: Public categories may include duplicate abuse, model-slot abuse, verification misuse, impersonation, fraud, automation abuse, and evasion attempts.

sections.body: Appeal access does not guarantee reversal, restored access, verification approval, badge restoration, or removal of every restriction.

pages.title: Terms Direction
pages.description: Terms direction documentation for accounts, profiles, canonical personal models, Posts, subscriptions, verification, safety restrictions, appeals, privacy, developer use, and public legal review boundaries.

hero.title: Terms Direction
hero.description: Terms direction defines public legal structure before final legal approval.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Terms direction defines public legal structure for account use, profiles, canonical personal models, Posts, subscriptions, verification, safety restrictions, developer access, privacy controls, support, and dispute handling.

sections.title: Review status
sections.body: Final terms require legal, governance, privacy, security, product, finance, support, infrastructure, and executive review.

pages.title: Model Identity
pages.description: Model identity documentation for one profile, one canonical personal model, continuity, anti-impersonation protection, and model identity governance.

hero.title: Model Identity
hero.description: Model identity documentation defines the relationship between profile identity, canonical personal model continuity, and trust boundaries.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Model identity keeps profile identity and canonical personal model continuity governed and protected.

sections.title: Core rule
sections.body: One profile should remain tied to one canonical personal model.

pages.title: One Profile One Canonical Model
pages.description: One profile one canonical model documentation for identity continuity, personal model governance, anti-impersonation protection, subscription boundaries, verification, abuse prevention, and model integrity.
pages.breadcrumb: Model Identity / One Profile One Canonical Model

hero.title: One Profile One Canonical Model
hero.description: Each Neuroartan profile is tied to one canonical personal model.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: The one-profile-one-model principle protects continuity and identity integrity.

sections.title: Subscription boundary
sections.body: Subscriptions may increase depth and controls, but they must not create additional canonical personal models.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: OAuth connectors give users a controlled way to authorize external services for approved Neuroartan functionality.

sections.title: Security
sections.body: Connector documentation must not expose tokens, client secrets, provider credentials, or private implementation details.

hero.description: API integration documentation defines approved endpoints, authentication, authorization, schemas, rate limits, error responses, and support-safe examples.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: API documentation explains approved integrations in a controlled, searchable, support-safe way.

sections.title: Boundary
sections.body: Draft endpoints, internal routing, private infrastructure paths, service keys, and abuse-sensitive controls must remain private.

pages.title: Security Requirements
pages.description: Security requirements documentation for developer integrations, OAuth, APIs, CLI, secrets, server-side boundaries, webhooks, rate limits, abuse-sensitive systems, and public-safe technical documentation.

hero.title: Security Requirements
hero.description: Security requirements define the public developer boundary for secrets, OAuth, APIs, CLI output, server-side operations, webhooks, rate limits, provider data, support diagnostics, and abuse-sensitive systems.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Security requirements define what must be protected before any developer-facing integration or public technical article is released.

sections.title: Secret handling
sections.body: Secrets must never appear in public documentation, frontend code, screenshots, support output, CLI examples, logs, copied commands, or issue reports.

hero.title: CLI
hero.description: The Neuroartan CLI is a future developer and operator interface for controlled diagnostics, build checks, deployment checks, documentation tasks, support-safe output, and traceable public errors.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: The CLI should provide controlled command-line workflows for diagnostics, build checks, deployment checks, documentation checks, and support-safe command output.

sections.title: Output standard
sections.body: CLI output should be direct, machine-readable where appropriate, human-readable by default, and safe for support use.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Troubleshooting documentation helps users and developers resolve public-facing issues without exposing private infrastructure or security-sensitive data.

sections.title: Article standard
sections.body: Every troubleshooting article should include a stable issue ID, affected surface, symptom, public-safe cause, recommended fix, verification step, and escalation condition.

sections.title: Code families

sections.title: Standard
